page: Microsoft Azure E16-8s_v6 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server instance belonging to the Esv6 family. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8573C processor operating at 4.2 GHz. The processor includes 2 threads per core, a 260 MB L3 cache, and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel AMX for tile and int8 operations. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high resource density of 16.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance, though it is Premium Storage capable for remote disk attachments.

Benchmark evaluations show strong single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1975.0 and single-threaded gzip compression reaching 31.02 GB/sec. Multi-core performance is moderate, as indicated by a stress-ng multi-core score of 7972.24 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 7558.0. Memory bandwidth tests demonstrate 626.49 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, but drop to 42.29 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads, contributing to a weak PassMark memory score of 1436.15. For database operations, the instance handles 11.31 million Redis SET operations per second and scores 4102.46 on PassMark database tests.

The high memory-to-vCPU ratio provides qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring large in-memory datasets without needing high vCPU counts. However, users face tradeoffs: the lack of local NVMe storage requires reliance on network-attached Premium Storage, and the system exhibits weak performance in larger LLM tasks, such as text generation on a medium 7B model and prompt processing on a large 70B model. Conversely, it performs strongly on smaller AI models, achieving 302.13 tokens/sec for text generation on a 135M model. This profile makes the instance suitable for relational databases, in-memory caching, text processing, and lightweight machine learning inference.
description: The Microsoft Azure E16-8s_v6 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server from the Esv6 family, featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz. It is configured with 128.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 16.0 GB per core, but includes no local storage or GPUs. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1975.0 and 31.02 GB/sec in single-threaded gzip compression. However, memory performance is a notable tradeoff, with a weak PassMark memory score of 1436.15 and reduced uncached large-block read bandwidth. The instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy workloads that do not require high compute density, making it suitable for database operations, in-memory caching, and text processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E16-8s_v6 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and no local storage, suitable for database operations and in-memory caching.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E16-8s_v6 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and support for Premium Storage.
tagline: An x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine featuring a high memory-to-core ratio, Intel Xeon processing, and dedicated vCPUs for database workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 4.2 GHz clock speed
128.0 GB of system memory providing 16.0 GB of RAM per core
No local storage, requiring network-attached Premium Storage
Strong single-core performance and small-model LLM text generation
Weak memory performance in PassMark benchmarks and large-block uncached reads
Best suited for in-memory databases, caching, and text processing workloads
